prompt: Create an ERC-20 token called GoldToken (GOLD) in my SE-2 project with capped supply of 1 million tokens, minting restricted to the owner, and a frontend page to mint and transfer tokens. Show token balances properly formatted.

assertions.text: Uses named imports for OpenZeppelin contracts: import {ERC20} from '...' syntax, NOT import '...' wildcard imports
assertions.rationale: OZ v5 requires named imports. Without the skill, model often uses wildcard import syntax.

assertions.text: Contract includes ERC20Permit extension (ERC-2612) for gasless approvals via off-chain signatures
assertions.rationale: Skill says ERC20Permit is 'effectively standard for new tokens now'. Without the skill, model rarely includes it unprompted.

assertions.text: Frontend reads decimals() from the contract and uses formatUnits(value, decimals) / parseUnits(amount, decimals) — NOT hardcoded formatEther/parseEther
assertions.rationale: Skill teaches using dynamic decimals for robustness. Without the skill, model defaults to formatEther/parseEther assuming 18 decimals.

assertions.text: Frontend has a centralized formatting helper function (e.g., formatGold, formatToken) rather than inline formatting scattered across the component
assertions.rationale: Skill's decimals guidance encourages a single formatting function. Without the skill, formatting is usually done inline at each display point.

assertions.text: The _update function override specifier is correctly scoped — lists only the parent contracts that actually override _update (not extra ones like ERC20Permit that don't)
assertions.rationale: With ERC20Permit added, a common mistake is including it in the override specifier even though it doesn't override _update in OZ v5.

assertions.text: Uses OpenZeppelin's ERC20Capped extension for max supply enforcement, not a manual cap implementation
assertions.rationale: This was the only discriminating assertion from iteration-4. Keep it.

assertions.text: Uses SE-2 hooks (useScaffoldReadContract/useScaffoldWriteContract) for contract interaction
assertions.rationale: Baseline assertion — should pass regardless. Included as sanity check.

assertions.text: Proper Hardhat deploy script exists in packages/hardhat/deploy/
assertions.rationale: Baseline assertion — should pass regardless. Included as sanity check.

assertions.text: Frontend page includes both mint and transfer functionality
assertions.rationale: Baseline assertion — should pass regardless. Included as sanity check.

assertions.text: Constructor uses Ownable(initialOwner) pattern (OZ v5), not parameterless Ownable()
assertions.rationale: Baseline assertion — should pass regardless. Included as sanity check.
